title: rosa × collina subsp. macrantha
intro: Rosa × collina subsp. macrantha, also known as the pink or wild dog rose, is a subspecies of rose bush native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are derived from its flowers, leaves, and stems. The health benefits of Rosa × collina subsp. macrantha include reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and soothing digestive issues, due to its ability to stimulate circulation and relax the muscles. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
